headline: Stress Management
description: Stress is the body's response to perceived threats. Acute stress (fight-or-flight) is adaptive; chronic stress damages health (cardiovascular, immune, mental). Management: exercise, mindfulness/meditation, social connection, time in nature, deep breathing, therapy (CBT). Zebras don't get ulcers because they turn stress responses off.

citation.name: Why Zebras Don't Get Ulcers (Robert Sapolsky, 3rd Ed)
